commands.review_summary:
The PR passes the requested `agentId` into `tools.effective` session loading and adds focused regression coverage for global sessions on non-default agents.

PR surface: Source +3, Tests +30. Total +33 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main and `v2026.6.10` load `global` in `tools.effective` without passing the requested agent id before comparing against the session agent; I did not run a live repro because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No automated repair is needed; the remaining blocker is contributor-supplied real behavior proof plus normal exact-head maintainer review.

target.title: fix(gateway): tools.effective fails for global sessions on non-default agents
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where operators viewing the Control UI tools panel for a **global** session on a **non-default agent** would get `agent id "<requested>" does not match session agent "<default>"` and see no effective tool list.

Repro: call `tools.effective` with `{ sessionKey: "global", agentId: "work" }` while `work` is a configured agent and the default agent is `main`.

## Why This Change Was Made

`tools.effective` already validates and resolves `requestedAgentId`, but `resolveTrustedToolsEffectiveContext` called `loadSessionEntry(sessionKey)` without passing that agent id. For `global` keys, that loads the default agent's store row, so the subsequent `resolveSessionAgentId` check compares the wrong agent and rejects the request.

The fix passes `{ agentId: requestedAgentId }` into `loadSessionEntry`, matching `sessions.get` and other gateway session RPCs. The post-load agent-id match guard is unchanged.

## User Impact

Operators can open the effective tools inventory for global sessions scoped to any configured agent (not only the default agent). No config or protocol changes.

## Evidence

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server-methods/tools-effective.test.ts
# Test Files  2 passed (2)
# Tests       42 passed (42)
```

New regression test `loads global sessions with the requested agent id before matching session agent` asserts:
- `loadSessionEntry("global", { agentId: "work" })` is used
- request succeeds and tool inventory resolves with `agentId: "work"`
